samples.xml_description:
Unsigned 8-bit multiplier with pipelined processing. Clock signal for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Enable signal for multiplication operation. Multiplicand. Multiplier. Output enable signal for multiplication operation. Product output. 8 9 {signal} is the set of all bit signals, within the design, that are assigned in some way, either directly or indirectly. This includes the output signals, wire declarations and the bits of all registers. The {control} set is the subset of {signals} that includes all the signals used for controlling the operation of the design, including control and state signals used in sequential logic. The {data} set is the subset of {signals} that includes all the signals used for data transfer and storage, including wire and register declarations. Finally, the {computed data} set is the subset of {data} that includes all signals that are assigned a value either directly or indirectly through a logic or arithmetic statement, including the bits of all registers and the result of any data or arithmetic operations. Describe the design logic here.

Initialize the pipeline registers and accumulators:

multiplier_8bit

on the positive edge of the clock.

combinational logic

Generate the partial products using the generate statement:

multiplier_8bit

Update the pipeline registers and accumulators on the positive edge of the clock:

multiplier_8bit

Output the final product:

multiplier_8bit
